title: pimelea sylvestris
intro: Pimelea sylvestris, also known as Bush Pea or Forest Rice Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia and Tasmania. It is a shrub that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves, stems, and flowers are used to make herbal preparations that offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Pimelea sylvestris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
